Details: Based on bench-scale studies [2749, 2759, 2767, 2775, 2776, 2779, 2791], chlorine appears to be an effective treatment for cylindrospermopsin (up to 100 percent removal). Effectiveness is a function of chlorine dose (ranged from 1 to 5 mg/L), contact time, and organic content, with higher doses and contract times generally leading to higher reported removals.
